data.text: Make me a tinder like app

Built a static Tinder-like app in [index.html](/home/navid/java/app-builder/agent-backend/workspaces/make-me-a-tinder-like-app-7c15/index.html): JWT auth, a BFF-backed swipe-style feed, post creation for profile cards, follow/unfollow as likes, comments as openers, search, and disabled placeholders for real matching features that the backend does not expose.

Backend wiring uses `GET /auth/me`, `POST /auth/login`, `POST /auth/register`, `GET /bff/feed`, `POST /posts`, `PUT/DELETE /posts/users/{userId}/follow`, `POST /comments/targets/post/{postId}`, `GET /comments/targets/post/{postId}/summary`, and `GET /post-search?q=...`. `./verify-backend.sh` now passes contract lint, but the live check is blocked in this environment at the CORS preflight step with `curl: (7) failed to open socket: Operation not permitted`, so I cannot claim end-to-end backend verification passed here.

data.userMessage: The app was built, but a real backend test failed, so I did not release the preview. Ask the builder to fix the backend verification report.

data.text:
I reviewed `AGENTS.md`, `.hermes/skills/plugs/SKILL.md`, and the current `index.html`. The existing frontend already stays within the allowed backend contract and wires `auth-service`, `bff`, `tweeter-service`, `comment-service`, and `post-search-service`, so I did not change the app files.

I ran `./verify-backend.sh`. The frontend contract lint passed, but the verifier stopped at the first live gateway check with `curl: (7) failed to open socket: Operation not permitted` during the CORS preflight to `http://localhost:18000/media/upload-intents`. So the real result is: static app unchanged, frontend contract valid, backend smoke verification blocked by sandbox network access before any app-specific failure could be observed.
